regime_justification: USDJPY hovering 0.46 yen below 160 intervention watch line, MOF vocal on decisive action, BOJ likely on hold Mon–Tue amid Iran uncertainty, yet carry spread still 187 bps and vol subdued—watch-list conditions active.

domino_stage.label: Pre-Intervention
domino_stage.justification: USDJPY at 159.54, 46 pips from 160 MOF watch line; Katayama renewed decisive-action warning Friday citing US coordination; risk reversals and basis data unavailable but FX vol low (7.72); BOJ meets Apr 27–28, market prices 7% hike but hold expected.

1w.label: Watchful
1w.driver: BOJ meeting Mon–Tue likely hold, MOF intervention rhetoric elevated, USDJPY ~160 fragile but vol still low; applied unwind prior (data ambiguous, would otherwise grade D).

2w.label: Watchful
2w.driver: Post-BOJ window: if hold + hawkish signal June, spread intact but intervention risk lingers; oil ~$95 WTI keeps ¥/bbl at 15,118, supporting 2% inflation fears.

3w.label: Watchful
3w.driver: Setup remains fragile; Iran ceasefire extension bought time but no resolution; energy prices elevated; if USDJPY pushes 161+ or BOJ signals June hike, triggers arm further.

4w.label: Unlikely
4w.driver: If BOJ holds, spreads persist, Iran tensions ease, and USDJPY oscillates 157–160 range without catalyst; June meeting is the next live hike window, pushing unwind risk out.

bottom_line: Pre-intervention posture: USDJPY 159.54, 46 pips from the 160 MOF red line. Katayama renewed decisive-action warnings with US coordination on Friday. BOJ meets Mon–Tue; 93% expect hold, but June hike now 57% priced. Oil ~$95 keeps import-cost pressure on. Carry spread 187 bps, vol still low (7.72 on Apr 17), VIX 18.7, MOVE 67. Grades C/C/C/D: watchful near-term, easing week-4 if no catalyst. The fragility is policy-path uncertainty plus proximity to the intervention line—not yet unwind, but the setup is cocked.

trigger_map.note: USDJPY 159.54, 46 pips from 160; Katayama Apr 23 renewed 'decisive action with US coordination'; rate-check plumbing from Jan still live.
Apr 27–28 meeting: 93% expect hold, 7% hike. June now 57% priced. Hold + hawkish signal likely arms June trigger; surprise hike would flip to firing.

trigger_map.trigger: JGB 10Y break above prior cycle high
trigger_map.note: JGB 10Y 2.44%, one-week high; inflation accelerated Mar 1.8%; if BOJ signals June hike, 2.5%+ breakout could cascade.
MOVE 67, well below 100; rates vol subdued despite geopolitical tail and Fed–BOJ divergence setup.
WTI $95, ¥/bbl 15,118; Iran ceasefire extended indefinitely but no formal talks; Hormuz still restricted; renewed spike would arm MOF intervention trigger.

narrative.p1: Friday's tape: USDJPY slipped 5 pips to 159.54, holding 46 pips below the 160 MOF intervention line. Katayama renewed decisive-action warnings, stressing US coordination as ceasefire extension bought time but Iran talks collapsed. WTI pulled back to $95 on diplomacy hopes but is still +14% for the week. JGB 10Y hit 2.44%, a one-week high, as March core inflation accelerated to 1.8% for the first time in five months. US 10Y touched 4.31%, a one-week high Thursday, before easing. VIX 18.7, MOVE 67, both low. Nikkei closed +0.97% at 59,716, nearing 60k. BTC $77,633, flat. No stage change from yesterday's yellow/Stage 2.
narrative.p2: Quant anchors: US–JP spread 187 bps (compressed ~7 bps week-on-week but still wide), USDJPY 1M IV 7.72 (Apr 17 data, vol subdued), ¥/bbl 15,118 (elevated but off peak). USDJPY 5-day % +0.03%, pace neutral; distance to 160 is 46 pips (0.29%), fragile. BOJ meets Mon–Tue Apr 27–28; market prices 7% hike, 93% hold. Consensus: hold, revise inflation higher, growth lower, June hike window opens. We remain Stage 2 Pre-Intervention: proximity to 160, MOF vocal, BOJ on hold but hawkish lean. 1w/2w grades C—applied unwind prior as tiebreaker; data is genuinely ambiguous (vol low, spreads intact, but price 46 pips from red line and MOF rhetoric elevated). Without the prior, 1w would grade D. 3w C (fragile setup persists), 4w D (if no catalyst, June becomes the live window and near-term risk eases).
narrative.p3: Crypto liquidity: BTC $77,633, -0.8%, range-bound despite equity strength (S&P +0.8% Fri to new highs). Risk-on in equities (Nikkei, S&P) not spilling into crypto; BTC open interest and DXY correlation data unavailable. XRP $1.44 flat. Interpretation: crypto is decoupled from the equity rally, suggesting risk appetite is sector-specific (AI, Japan exporters) rather than broad liquidity expansion. BTC holding mid-70s without conviction. For positioning: if carry unwinds (JPY spike, equity selloff), BTC likely drains alongside risk assets. Current setup offers no edge; wait for volatility regime change or clear liquidity signal.

horizon_rationale.w1_w2: Week 1: BOJ meets Mon–Tue, likely hold but June hike signal would keep intervention risk elevated and USDJPY fragile near 160. Week 2: post-BOJ window; if hold + hawkish, market reprices June, spreads compress modestly, MOF remains vocal. Both weeks grade C—setup fragile, vol low, but no imminent catalyst unless BOJ surprises or USDJPY breaks 160.
horizon_rationale.w2_w3: Week 2 data-driven (post-BOJ), Week 3 scenario-weighted. If BOJ holds and signals June, intervention risk persists but unwind trigger pushed to June meeting (6 weeks out). If Iran talks restart and oil eases, ¥/bbl pressure abates, MOF rhetoric cools. Week 3 C holds if setup remains fragile; downgrades to D if oil/USDJPY stabilize and June hike priced smoothly.
horizon_rationale.w3_w4: Week 3 fragile (C), Week 4 eases to D. By week 4 (late May), if BOJ held in April, spreads intact, and USDJPY oscillates 157–160 without catalyst, unwind risk defers to June 15–16 BOJ meeting. Grade D reflects base case: carry persists, intervention rhetoric fades if price behaves, June is the next live catalyst. Non-monotonic path C/C/C/D valid—risk elevated near-term, eases if no trigger fires.

watch_list: BOJ decision Tue Apr 28: hold vs. hike; hawkish signal for June would keep grades C.
USDJPY break above 160.00: would likely trigger MOF intervention or rate-check within hours.
US–Iran talks: formal restart (via Pakistan) would ease oil, ¥/bbl, and MOF intervention pressure.
JGB 10Y above 2.50%: would signal market pricing BOJ acceleration, potential for vol cascade.
WTI above $100 or below $90: spike arms energy trigger, pullback eases import-cost narrative and intervention risk.
